criteria.reason: Inventory artifacts still cover the 35 active journal templates.
Implementation mapping remains documented in the inventory and implementation matrix, including the previously risky shared journal routes.
Live/source mapping artifacts still cover the active 35-journal set and include the fixed sanitation1journal alias.

Visual review proof is still incomplete and internally inconsistent: the reviewed visual matrix marks all 35 journals blocked, while only two batch-review artifacts record reviewed outcomes for 12 journals.
The bundle shows some visual parity progress, but it still does not provide a complete 35-journal reviewed verdict matrix that distinguishes matched, fixed, and blocked outcomes journal by journal.

Server-backed PostgreSQL evidence materially improves this criterion: the real dataset contains admin@haccp.local and a demo organization with 74 journal documents, proving the removed shared delete branch was a real data-loss risk on the actual server-backed flow. However, the task still lacks packaged end-to-end runtime proof for loading, saving, editing, and persistence across the full 35-journal set.

Server-backed evidence materially strengthens create/open reasoning because it proves the shared disappearing-document bug affected the real demo dataset, not just a hypothetical local seed. Cross-journal button wiring is documented, but the current artifacts still do not prove runtime correctness for every critical action across all 35 journals.

Code-level print divergence is cleaned up and existing live proof shows non-empty PDF downloads for print-expected journals, but the current repository proof still does not show application-runtime print verification journal by journal for the active 35.
The discovered defect classes were rechecked across the active 35, and the destructive list-route defect was fixed in shared code rather than patched journal by journal.
Required proof-loop artifacts remain present and validation passes.
This verifier pass used the current repository state and fresh command runs, including proof-loop validation plus current TypeScript and focused lint checks.
Completion cannot be claimed because AC4 and AC5 still fail, and AC6 through AC8 remain unproven at runtime.
